m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main( )
    {
      int i=0;
     for(;i<=3; printf("%d " ++i ) );
    }
m_Item1.Explanation:
Iteration 1:i=0 0<=3 true, print ++i i.e 1
Iteration 2:i=1 1<=3 true, print ++i i.e 2
Iteration 3:i=2 2<=3 true, print ++i i.e 3
Iteration 4:i=3 3<=3 true, print ++i i.e 4
Iteration 5:i=4 4<=3 false so terminate the loop

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What will be output if you will compile and execute the following c code?
void main()
{
  int x;
  for(x=1;x<=5;x++);
   printf("%d",x);
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
for loop is terminated with semicolon means no body
step 1:x=1 x<=5 true and x will be incremented to 2
step 2:x=2 x<=5 true and x will be incremented to 3
step 3:x=3 x<=5 true and x will be incremented to 4
step 4:x=4 x<=5 true and x will be incremented to 5
step 5:x=5 x<=5 true and x will be incremented to 6
at last print statement will execute and i=6 will be printed

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include < stdio.h >
int main()
{
    int tally=0;
    for(;;)
    {
        if(tally==10)
            break;
        printf("%d ",++tally);
    }
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: for(; ;) it is possible in c, there is no need to place condition with in the for(), you can place condition with in the body of the loop.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include <stdio.h>
int main()
{
    int i=1;
    while(i<=10 && 1++)
        printf("Hello");
}

Options.OptionText: Error: lvalue required as increment operand

m_Item1.Explanation: you can not increment or decrement constant values directly.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of C Program.?
int main()
{
    int a=32;
    
    do
    {
        printf("%d ", a);
        a++;
        if(a > 35)
            break;
    }while(1);

    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: while(1) is infinite loop. So we kept if(condition) to break the loop. a++ is equivalent to a=a+1;

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of C Program.?
int main()
{
    int k, j;
    for(k=1, j=10; k <= 5; k++)
        printf("%d ", (k+j));
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
You can initialize any number of variables inside for loop.
Initially k =1 and j= 10 hence k+j  is 11
loop execute till k becomes <= 5 hence output will be  11 12 13 14 15

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is output of below program?
#include <stdio.h>
int main()
{
    int i, j;
    for (i = 0, j = 0; i < 5; i++)
    {
        printf("%d%d--", i, j);
    }
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: In this code only i is incrementing starting from 0 to 4 but j is 0 only.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which loop will not output nos. 1 to 10
m_Item1.Explanation:
for(a=0; a<10; a+1) - here in updation value of a is not getting updated as a+1 will not update a it is just adding 1 but the value of a remain same as we have not wriiten a=a+1 ,
so it will go in infinite loop & always print only 0;

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int main()
{
    int s = 0;
    while (s++<10)
     {
          if (s<4 && s<9)
              continue;
          printf("%d ", s);
     }
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Initially s is 0 and it is post incremented in while loop.  Inside while loop we have if condition which will continue the while loop till s<4. Hence once s become 4 , if condition return false and next printf statement get executed till s is 10. Hence output will be 4 5 6 7 8 9 10.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main ()
{
  static int i;
  int j;
  for (j = 0; j <= 5; j += 2)
    switch (j)
      {
      case 1:
    i++;
    break;

      case 2:
    i += 2;

      case 4:
    i %= 2;
    j = -1;
    continue;

      default:
    --i;
    continue;
      }
  printf ("%d", i);
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Compilation error due to continue in switch case.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int main()
{
int n, i;

printf("Enter the no. of loops to run:");
scanf("%d", &n);

for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
{
    printf("%d ", i);
}
 }
m_Item1.Explanation:
loop starts from 0 it will execute till i<n.
so it will print values from 0 to n-1

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main()
{
int j;
for(j=0; j<5; j++);
     printf("CDAC");
printf("ACTS");
}
m_Item1.Explanation: The semi-colon at the end of for loop hence for loop completes its execution first and then print CDACACTS.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main()
{
           int i=0;
for(;i<6;)
{
printf("%d",i);
i+=2;
}
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
step1:i=0;i<6 so it will print 0 and then i=i+2 i.e 2
step2:i=2;2<6 so it will print 2 and then i=i+2 i.e 4
step3:i=4;4<6 so it will print 4 and then i=i+2 i.e 6
step4:i=6;6<6 false hence now loop will be terminated

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main()
{
int i=0;

while(i);
printf("%d",i++);
}
m_Item1.Explanation: while loop is terminated by semicolon as well as initial value of i is 0 so it will execute once and get terminated. Also in print statement post-increment is used it means use the variable first and then increment so output is 0.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main()
{
int i=0;
while(printf("%d",i++))
printf("Hello");
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Their is no terminating condition for while loop.So it will go in infinite loop

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main ()
{
  int i = 0;
  while (i < 6);
          printf ("%d", ++i);
}
m_Item1.Explanation: there is semicolon given after while loop and their is no any terminating condition in while loop hence it will run infinitely.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of C Program.?


int main()
{
    while(true)    
    {
        printf("RABBIT");
        break;
    }
    
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: while(TRUE) or while(true) does not work. true is not a keyword.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int main()
{
    static int i;
    for (++i; ++i; ++i)
    {
        printf("%d ", i);
        if (i == 4)
break;
    }

    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
for loop executes only  twice. As the value of i is incremented in condition statement also. When i become 4 it will end the loop.
i=2, output 2.
i=4, output 4 and brak the loop.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
void main( )
    {
      int  i=0;
     while(++i < 5)
              printf("%d", ++i);
    }
m_Item1.Explanation:
every time we are pre incrementing i hence it will print 2 4. Initially i was 0 then we are pre incrementing i in while as well as in printf hence i becomes 2 and it  will print 2 then again  we are pre incrementing i in while as well as in printf hence i become 4 and it will print 4 then in while loop i become 5 which is not < 5 hence it will come out of while loop.

step 1:++i<5 i.e 1<5 is true so print ++i i.e 2
step 2:again ++i<5 i.e 3<5 is true so print ++i i.e 4
step 3:again ++i<5 i.e 5<5 is false so loop will be terminated

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int i = 40;
extern int i;
int main()
{
    do {
        printf("%d", i++);
    }   while (5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 0);
    return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: In while when you will have , seperated values it will consider the right most value which is 0. 0 means false. hence do while loop execute only once.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
int main ()
{
  int i = 0;
  for (++i; ++i; ++i)
    {
      printf ("%d", i);
      if (i == 4)
        break;
    }
  return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
step 1: ++i =1 ++i=2 which is true so 2 will be printed 
i==4 false
so again for loop will be executed
step 1: ++i =3 ++i=4 which is true so 4 will be printed 
i==4 true hence loop will break now.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which loop does not require to write ‘;’ after loop condition?
m_Item1.Explanation: while loop does not require to write loop condition. In Do while we have to give ; after while and in for loop also we are giving ; after condition and then write incrementation or decrementetion of variable.
